text:
Once upon a time in a quaint little town called Cherrywood, nestled between rolling hills and whispering forests, lived a peculiar fellow named Harold. Harold didn't look like anyone special. He had an unusually square hat that sat peculiarly atop his head, spectacles perched on bridges of flesh at his bridge-ruby nose. His laugh sounded like the tinkling of wind chimes.

Every weekday, Harold would trudge to his ancient wood-burning stove to brew his morning coffee. His breakfast was simple: toast with jam, a pot of whatever suspiciously brown tea he happened to have brew’d, and a candied orange peel he found in his garden.

One peculiar morning, Harold leaned heavily on his worn hoe, his chest puffing up with chestnut-colored breath. He decided to walk to the town library instead of his usual route. There were so many books he wanted to read.

At the library, Harold discovered a dusty tome about fairy legends. His eyes danced with excitement.
